skill.name: pexoai-agent
skill.description: Use this skill when the user wants to produce a short video (5–120 seconds). Supports any video type: product ads, TikTok/Instagram/YouTube content, brand videos, explainers, social clips. USE FOR: video production, AI video, make a video, product video, brand video, promotional clip, explainer video, short video.
skill.summary: AI video creation agent that handles scriptwriting, shot composition, transitions, and music for short videos.
skill.author: pexoai

security_audit.summary: This is a legitimate video generation API client for pexo.ai. Static analysis detected 534 potential issues, but evaluation reveals these are mostly documentation examples (showing users what commands to run) and standard shell scripting patterns for API operations. The skill makes network calls only to pexo.ai, uses environment variables only for its own API credentials (PEXO_API_KEY), and performs standard file operations for configuration and asset uploads. No malicious behavior, data exfiltration, or command injection vulnerabilities were found. All findings are consistent with expected behavior for a video API client tool.

medium_findings.title: Documentation Examples Flagged as Commands
medium_findings.description: Static scanner flagged command examples in documentation files (SETUP-CHECKLIST.md, TROUBLESHOOTING.md) as shell execution. These are documentation showing users what commands to run, not actual code execution points. VERDICT: FALSE POSITIVE - These are user-facing documentation examples.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Documentation files contain markdown code blocks showing users what commands to run for setup and troubleshooting. The backtick notation in markdown is documentation syntax, not actual shell execution.

medium_findings.title: Shell Command Substitution in Scripts
medium_findings.description: Scripts use $(command) substitution for legitimate API operations: extracting asset IDs, reading config files, file metadata. VERDICT: FALSE POSITIVE - Standard shell scripting patterns for a legitimate API client.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Command substitutions in scripts are used for: mktemp (temp files), cat (reading response files), jq (JSON parsing), basename/stat (file operations). These are standard patterns for API client operations.

low_findings.title: MD5 Referenced in Documentation
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 'weak cryptographic algorithm' at references/TROUBLESHOOTING.md:87 referencing md5. This is documentation showing users how to verify file checksums, not actual cryptographic use. VERDICT: FALSE POSITIVE - Non-security use of MD5 for file integrity checks.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: MD5 is documented as a file checksum utility (md5 file.mp4), not for cryptographic security. This is standard documentation for verifying downloaded file integrity.

low_findings.title: API Key Configuration in User Config File
low_findings.description: Skill requires PEXO_API_KEY to be stored in ~/.pexo/config. This is standard API client credential management. VERDICT: LOW RISK - User-managed credentials with clear documentation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Config file storage for API keys is standard practice. Documentation clearly instructs users to keep credentials secure. Skill does not transmit credentials to third parties.

content.user_title: Create professional short videos with AI
content.value_statement: Creating short videos manually is time-consuming and requires technical skills. Pexo AI Video Agent handles scriptwriting, shot composition, transitions, and music production automatically, producing professional 5-120 second videos in about 15-20 minutes.
actual_capabilities: Create 5-120 second videos from text descriptions
Support multiple aspect ratios: 16:9, 9:16, 1:1 for different platforms
Upload reference images, videos, or audio to guide video creation
Handle multi-turn workflows with preview options and user feedback
Automatically add music, transitions, and cinematic effects
Generate videos in multiple languages based on user input
limitations: Cannot generate videos longer than 120 seconds
Requires paid Pexo account with credits for video generation
Cannot crawl web URLs for assets; local files only
Processing time is 15-20+ minutes per video depending on complexity

use_cases.title: Marketing Teams Create Product Videos
use_cases.description: Marketing teams use this to quickly generate product demo videos, brand advertisements, and promotional clips for social media without hiring video editors.

use_cases.title: Content Creators Produce Social Media Clips
use_cases.description: YouTubers, TikTok creators, and Instagram influencers generate short clips with professional transitions and music to accompany their content.

use_cases.title: Developers Integrate Video in Applications
use_cases.description: Developers integrate Pexo into their workflows via CLI scripts to programmatically generate video content for user-facing applications.

prompt_templates.title: Simple Video Request
prompt_templates.prompt: I want a product video for my sneakers

prompt_templates.title: Detailed Video with Style
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 30-second brand video for my coffee shop featuring warm lighting, relaxed jazz music, and shots of people enjoying coffee
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate - includes duration, style, and specific requirements

prompt_templates.title: Video with Reference Images
prompt_templates.prompt: Make an explainer video using this product photo as the main visual <original-image>asset_id</original-image>, showing how it works in daily life

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Part Campaign
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a series of three 15-second Instagram Reels: (1) product unboxing, (2) lifestyle shot, (3) call-to-action with brand colors blue and white
prompt_templates.scenario: Expert - complex multi-scene request with specific platform targeting

output_examples.input: I want a product video for my sneakers
output_examples.output: Your video is being created! Estimated time: 15-20 minutes. Project: https://pexo.ai/project/proj_abc123

output_examples.input: Make a 20-second TikTok video about summer fashion
output_examples.output: Preview options available: [A] Beach scene with models [B] Urban street style [C] Tropical paradise. Which do you prefer?

output_examples.input: The first option looks great, please continue with that
output_examples.output: Your video is ready! Download: https://pexo-assets.oss-us-east-1.aliyuncs.com/projects/123/assets/video.mp4?Signature=xxx. Project: https://pexo.ai/project/proj_abc123

best_practices: Be specific about video style, mood, and target platform in your request
Upload reference images or videos when you have specific visual requirements
Respond promptly to Pexo questions to keep the workflow moving
anti_patterns: Do not add detailed video parameters (duration, aspect ratio, style) unless specifically requested - Pexo optimizes these professionally
Do not interrupt the polling process - wait for the 15-20 minute generation to complete
Do not modify asset tags or remove the <original-image> wrappers around uploaded file references

faq.question: How long does video generation take?
faq.answer: Short 15-second videos take 15-20 minutes. Longer or more complex videos may take longer.

faq.question: What aspect ratios are supported?
faq.answer: Videos can be created in 16:9 (YouTube), 9:16 (TikTok/Instagram Reels), or 1:1 (Instagram Feed) formats.

faq.question: Can I upload my own images or videos?
faq.answer: Yes, you can upload images (jpg, png, webp), videos (mp4, mov), or audio files. These are referenced using asset tags in your message.

faq.question: How do I revise a video?
faq.answer: Send your feedback in the same project. Pexo will use your existing video as reference and apply your changes.

faq.question: Why am I getting a credit error?
faq.answer: Video generation requires Pexo credits. Visit pexo.ai/home to purchase credits if you see an insufficient credits message.

faq.question: What file types can I upload?
faq.answer: Images: jpg, png, webp, bmp, tiff, heic. Videos: mp4, mov, avi. Audio: mp3, wav, aac, m4a, ogg, flac.
